headline: Chinese School Building Fund Raises RM1.8B While Government Allocates RM200M
context: Chinese education associations raised RM1.8B through community donations between 2020 and 2024, while federal government allocation for Chinese school infrastructure totalled RM200M.

cards.big: Chinese school communities raised RM1.8B in 5 years. Government gave RM200M.
cards.sub: Community funding is 9 times government allocation. For a constitutionally guaranteed education stream.

cards.big: Article 12(1) prohibits discrimination in education funding.
cards.sub: Per-student capital allocation for SJKC is RM680. For SK it is RM2,100. The ratio is 1:3.

cards.big: No new SJKC has been built in Peninsular Malaysia since 1999.
cards.sub: Population-shifted areas like Puchong and Setia Alam have waiting lists of 800+ students for existing schools.

cards.big: SJKC student population grew 12% in 10 years.
cards.sub: 28% of students are now non-Chinese — Malay, Indian, and others choosing Chinese-medium education.

cards.big: A system where citizens must fundraise RM1.8B for constitutionally protected schools is not community spirit. It is state neglect rebranded.
Education funding should follow the student, not the stream. Equal per-student allocation across all school types would be constitutionally required, fiscally neutral, and politically overdue.
